examples: She bent down to the Narcissus.
Narcissus met a nasty end, of course.
You remember the Greek myth of Narcissus, who fell in love with his own image.
When a preening guest approaches the mirror, the surface illuminates to reveal tiny, iridescent Botox bottles with preserved narcissus flowers.
The myth of Narcissus, for example, tells of the beautiful young man whose problem was not that he loved himself but that he couldn't love himself.
